title: Redis credentials should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Redis connection URL with embedded password detected in source code. Redis connection strings in format redis://[user]:password@host:port expose authentication credentials that grant full access to cached data, session storage, and real-time application state. Exposing these credentials allows attackers to read sensitive cached data, manipulate session information, inject malicious cached content, execute arbitrary Redis commands, flush entire databases, and potentially pivot to other services. IMMEDIATELY rotate this password in your Redis server configuration, update all application configurations to use secure credential storage (environment variables, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, HashiCorp Vault), review Redis access logs for unauthorized activity, audit cached data for tampering, and implement proper access controls with Redis ACLs.

Redis password detected in configuration. Redis authentication passwords protect access to cached data, session stores, and real-time application state. Exposed passwords allow unauthorized access to sensitive cached information, session hijacking, data manipulation, command execution, and database flushing. Store Redis passwords securely using environment variables or secret management systems.

Redis requirepass directive with password detected. The requirepass directive in redis.conf sets the authentication password for Redis server access. Hardcoded requirepass values in configuration files or scripts expose Redis credentials, enabling unauthorized access to all cached data, session information, and Redis administrative functions. Store Redis passwords in secure configuration management systems and use environment variable substitution.
